risk_domain.name: Stealth Address Operational Privacy
risk_domain.description: Operational heuristics that deanonymize stealth address transactions despite correct cryptographic construction

H1_same_entity_withdrawal.name: Same-entity withdrawal
H1_same_entity_withdrawal.description: Sender and receiver belong to the same address cluster. Trivial deanonymization if the withdrawal address has any transaction history with the sender.

signals.name: shared_cluster
signals.description: Withdrawal address appears in same address cluster as deposit address

signals.name: common_funding
signals.description: Withdrawal and deposit addresses funded by same source

signals.name: ens_linkage
signals.description: Both addresses resolve to same ENS domain or are in same ENS ownership

detection.threshold: any signal above 0.7 confidence triggers alert

recommendations.description: Use a freshly generated address with no on-chain history for withdrawal
Withdraw to a different L2 or sidechain with no history linkage

H1_same_entity_withdrawal.fundamental_limitation: Address clustering improves over time. A fresh address today may be linked to the user's cluster in the future through subsequent activity.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Generate N deposit/withdrawal pairs where withdrawal address is in sender's cluster
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of pairs where adversary correctly links deposit to withdrawal
benchmark_scenario.baseline: ~95% linkable (near certain if cluster is known)

H2_gas_price_fingerprinting.name: Gas price fingerprinting
H2_gas_price_fingerprinting.description: Consistent or unusual gas price settings create a behavioral fingerprint across stealth address transactions.

signals.name: gas_outlier
signals.description: Gas price/priority fee is >2 std devs from block median

signals.name: gas_consistency
signals.description: Same exact gas parameters across multiple stealth transactions

signals.name: gas_pattern
signals.description: Gas price follows a recognizable pattern (e.g., always round numbers, always +5% above base)

recommendations.description: Set gas price randomly within the [25th, 75th] percentile of recent block gas distribution
Use wallet's default gas estimation (most common fingerprint = least identifiable)

H2_gas_price_fingerprinting.fundamental_limitation: Gas randomization only works if other users also have diverse gas patterns. If most wallets use identical gas estimation, any deviation is a fingerprint.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Pool of N users with varying gas behaviors. Adversary sees 10 stealth txs per user.
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary accuracy in attributing stealth txs to users based on gas fingerprint alone
benchmark_scenario.baseline: depends on user gas diversity -- high for custom gas users

H3_timing_correlation.name: Timing correlation
H3_timing_correlation.description: Spending from a stealth address shortly after receiving narrows the anonymity set to other deposits in the same time window.

signals.name: short_dwell
signals.description: Time between deposit receipt and first spend is < 1 hour

signals.name: consistent_dwell
signals.description: User consistently spends within similar time windows across stealth addresses

detection.threshold: short_dwell triggers BLOCK, medium_dwell triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Wait a random period (6-24 hours) before spending from stealth address
Time spending to coincide with high-activity periods (more deposits = larger anonymity set)

H3_timing_correlation.fundamental_limitation: With few stealth address users, even a 24h delay may not provide sufficient anonymity set. This heuristic cannot be fully defeated by technology alone -- requires more users.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Stream of N deposits over 24h. Adversary sees deposit and withdrawal timestamps.
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary's ability to match deposit-withdrawal pairs using timing windows
benchmark_scenario.baseline: 48.5% of Umbra txs linkable (paper result, all heuristics combined)

H4_funding_linkability.name: Funding linkability
H4_funding_linkability.description: The stealth address needs gas to operate. If gas comes from a known address, the stealth address is deanonymized.

signals.name: direct_funding
signals.description: Stealth address received ETH for gas directly from a known address

signals.name: relay_funding
signals.description: Stealth address funded through a single intermediary from known address

recommendations.description: Use ERC-4337 paymaster to pay gas, hiding msg.sender entirely
Use a relayer/meta-transaction to submit on behalf of stealth address
Fund gas from a fresh address with no history (e.g., CEX withdrawal to new address)

H4_funding_linkability.fundamental_limitation: Paymaster usage itself may become a fingerprint if few users use paymasters. The anonymity set of paymaster users matters.

benchmark_scenario.setup: N stealth addresses with various gas funding patterns
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of stealth addresses linkable to an identity via gas funding chain
benchmark_scenario.baseline: most Umbra users fund gas from known addresses

H5_self_transfer.name: Same sender = recipient
H5_self_transfer.description: User sends to their own stealth address. Trivial to detect and fully deanonymizing.

signals.name: self_send
signals.description: Transaction sender address matches stealth address registrant

recommendations.description: Prevent the transaction from being submitted. There is no privacy-preserving self-transfer.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Include self-transfer attempts in test set

H6_unique_amounts.name: Unique amounts
H6_unique_amounts.description: Non-standard transfer amounts (e.g., 1.037 ETH) are unique identifiers. If only one deposit of that amount exists in the time window, timing + amount = certain match.

signals.name: rare_amount
signals.description: Transfer amount has <5 matches in the stealth address deposit pool within 24h

signals.name: precise_amount
signals.description: Amount has >4 decimal places (not a round number)

signals.name: unique_in_pool
signals.description: Exact amount appears only once in the deposit pool

detection.threshold: unique_in_pool triggers BLOCK, rare_amount triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Split into multiple round-number transfers across different time windows
Check current deposit pool and choose an amount that matches existing deposits
recommendations.user_cost: may not transfer exact desired amount

H6_unique_amounts.fundamental_limitation: Amount normalization reduces precision. For exact payments, splitting and timing are the only options.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Deposit pool of N transactions with realistic amount distribution. Adversary matches by amount + timing.
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary accuracy in linking deposits to withdrawals

timing_delay.description: Queue transaction for delayed submission after random interval

gas_randomizer.description: Set gas parameters randomly within normal distribution of recent blocks

paymaster.description: Submit UserOperation through paymaster to hide msg.sender

amount_normalizer.description: Round transfer amount to nearest standard denomination

pool_monitor.description: Read current deposit pool to suggest amounts that blend in

activity_monitor.description: Monitor stealth address activity to suggest high-traffic windows for spending

relay.description: Submit transaction via relayer to hide stealth address as msg.sender

transfer_splitter.description: Split a transfer into multiple round-number amounts across different time windows

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring deanonymization rate with all heuristics applied

methodology.step_1: Generate synthetic dataset of N stealth address transactions with realistic distributions
methodology.step_2: Run adversary simulator applying all 6 heuristics
methodology.step_3: Measure baseline deanonymization rate (expect ~48.5% matching paper)
methodology.step_4: Apply profile recommendations to all transactions
methodology.step_5: Re-run adversary simulator on modified transactions
methodology.step_6: Compare: deanon rate before vs after

templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} stealth address transactions. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This reduces deanonymization risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
